changeSet.author: nexus-ykzbj.8

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE n.nspname = 'nexus' AND c.relname = 'diag_chash_conformance'

changeSet.comment: SELECT-only grants for the nexus_diag diagnostic role (RDR-182 P2.1). Conditional on the role existing (clean skip otherwise; self-heals every startup via runAlways). ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES runs as this migration connection's role (nexus_admin — the creator of every schema table), so future tables are covered automatically. Read-only by construction: SELECT on tables plus the Liquibase journal; deliberately NO sequence usage, NO DML, NO EXECUTE on the soft-delete functions.

changeSet.author: nexus-9bufb

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE n.nspname = 'nexus' AND c.relname = 'diag_chash_conformance'

changeSet.comment: Amendment A6 (nexus-9bufb): VIEW-era grants. Once the superuser provisioning path has created nexus.diag_chash_conformance, the diagnostic role's content boundary becomes structural: revoke direct table SELECT (nexus_admin owns the tables and made the original grants, so it can revoke them), keep schema USAGE, the counts view, and the Liquibase journal. Idempotent + runAlways: self-heals every boot, and re-revokes anything the legacy changeset era granted.
